analysis.note: Predicate nominative neuter in the quoted clause with idam; the accusative would be formally identical, but the commentary paraphrases it as the assertion “this is objectless; no external object exists.”

verification.detail: compound stem vijñaptimātra as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Final -a coalesces with initial i- of idam in the printed sandhi-unit evedam.
Neuter nominative subject of the quoted assertion; its initial i- participates in eva + idam → evedam.
Quotation-marker; before api it appears as ity.
The bhāṣya reading is api hi, with hi + upalambhataḥ printed as hy upalambhataḥ.
Ablatival/tasil-type indeclinable from upalambha, not a regular a-stem subanta; Sthiramati glosses it by grahaṇataḥ and citrīkaraṇataḥ.

Masculine nominative singular of the causative present active participial stem sthāpayant: “placing/setting.” It takes kiñcit as its object and shares the masculine singular subject implied by the commentary’s ābhimānikaḥ.
Commentary fixes the sense as abhimukham, “in front/facing,” not “first/in the beginning.”

Neuter accusative singular object of sthāpayan; the commentary illustrates it with neuter accusative practice-supports such as āsthisaṃkalikam and vinīlakam. The citation lemma kaścit is not used here as the derivational stem, since the neuter singular form is kiñcit.
Locative governed by avatiṣṭhate. Here tat refers back to vijñapti/vijñaptimātratā; this is not the Sāṃkhya “subtle element.”

verification.detail: compound stem tanmātra as Napumsaka/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Coalesces with following avatiṣṭhate in the printed sandhi-unit nāvatiṣṭhate.
Ātmanepada present 3rd singular of ava-√sthā, “to abide/stand”; root is recorded bare with prefix separately. Its initial a coalesces with na in nāvatiṣṭhate.

justifications.decision: Read the verse as a corrective to ābhimāna based on mere hearing; this controls the concessive “even” in the translation.
options_considered: The subject is an accomplished yogin established in vijñaptimātratā.
The subject is a conceited person whose claim to establishment must be rejected.
justifications.chosen: The verse describes a conceited learner who thinks he is established in purified representation-only, not someone who has genuinely attained that state.

evidence.quote:
iti atha vā yaḥ punar ābhimānikaḥ śrutamātrakeṇa jānīyad ahaṃ
vijñaptimātratāyāṃ śuddhāyāṃ sthita iti tadgrahavyudāsārtham āha
evidence.translation: Or else: someone conceited might, from mere hearing, think, “I am established in purified representation-only”; to remove that grasp, he says...

evidence.quote:
vijñaptimātram evedam ity api hy upalambhata ity
ādi
evidence.translation: ...the verse beginning “Even [thinking] ‘This is mere representation’—for he apprehends...”

justifications.decision: Translate vijñaptimātram evedam as “This is mere representation alone,” not as a mere quantity of information or as a claim compatible with external objects.
options_considered: “vijñapti-mātra” as representation plus an external object.
justifications.chosen: vijñapti-mātra means objectless “mere representation,” excluding a bāhyārtha, an external object.

evidence.quote: vijñaptimātram evedaṃ artharahitaṃ na bāhyo 'rtho 'stīty evam
evidence.translation: “This is mere representation, objectless; there is no external object”—in this way...

justifications.decision: Render with “as he apprehends—grasps and pictures,” while recognizing that this apprehension is precisely what has not yet been abandoned.
options_considered: upalambhataḥ as perception of a real external object.
justifications.chosen: upalambhataḥ denotes problematic apprehension/grasping/mental picturing and supplies the reason he does not abide in representation-only.

evidence.translation: upalambhataḥ means grahaṇataḥ, “from/by grasping,” and citrīkaraṇataḥ, “from/by making a mental image.”
Because the apprehension of consciousness has not been abandoned.

justifications.decision: Take agrataḥ as “in front/facing,” sthāpayan as a mental act, and kiñcit as one among the many Yogācāra supports, not a real external thing placed physically before the practitioner.
options_considered: sthāpayan as physically placing an external object.
kiñcit as an unspecified real external thing.
A mental placing-before of one among many practice-supports.
justifications.chosen: agrataḥ sthāpayan kiñcit means mentally setting some practice-support before himself.

evidence.translation: sthāpayan means mentally, just as it has been heard/taught.

evidence.quote:
bahuprakāratvād yogācārālambanānāṃ kiṃcid ity āhāsthisaṃkalikaṃ vā vinīlakaṃ
vā vipūyakaṃ vā vipaḍumakaṃ vā vyādhmātakādikaṃ vā
evidence.translation: Because Yogācāra supports are of many kinds, by “something” he means, for example, a skeleton-support, a livid one, a festering one, a discolored one, a bloated one, and so on.

justifications.decision: Connect tanmātra back to vijñaptimātratā rather than reading it as a Sāṃkhya subtle element or as “in that object alone.”
options_considered: tanmātra as a Sāṃkhya subtle element.
tanmātra as the mere representation-only just under discussion.
justifications.chosen: tanmātre is the locative “in that mere [representation],” i.e. in representation-only, and nāvatiṣṭhate is failure to be established there.

evidence.translation: He does not abide in that mere [representation], because the apprehension of consciousness has not been abandoned.

justifications.decision: Use the bhāṣya’s api hy upalambhataḥ rather than the vulgate-like hayu sequence.
options_considered: api hi / api hy, the bhāṣya reading.
api hayu, the vulgate variant as transmitted in the prompt.
justifications.chosen: The bhāṣya reading is api hi, yielding “for even,” with hi sandhied to hy before upalambhataḥ.

evidence.quote: vijñaptimātram evedam ity api hy upalambhata ity

translation:
For even as he apprehends—grasps and pictures—
“This is mere representation (vijñapti-mātra) alone,”
mentally setting some support (ālambana) before him,
he does not abide in that mere [representation] (tanmātra).
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind translation might make this verse praise the cognition “all this is vijñapti-mātra”; Sthiramati says it targets an ābhimānika relying on mere hearing.
It might treat the “something” placed in front as a real external object; the commentary makes it a mentally set practice-support among Yogācāra ālambanas.
It might render upalambhataḥ as neutral or successful perception; Sthiramati glosses it as grahaṇa/citrīkaraṇa and says the problem is non-abandonment of vijñānopalambha.
It might miss that vijñapti-mātra excludes external objects; the commentary explicitly glosses it as artharahita and says no bāhyārtha exists.
It might take tanmātra as a Sāṃkhya subtle element or “that object alone”; the commentary ties it to abiding in purified vijñaptimātratā.
analyzer_disagreements: ByT5 splits vijñaptimātram into vijñapti + mātram; I record the whole inflected compound stem vijñaptimātra, as required for compounds and as supported by the commentary’s vijñaptimātratā and gloss of vijñaptimātram.
ByT5/local segmentation gives evā/eva around evedam; I analyze the printed form as regular eva + idam with a+i → e, not as an independent evā.

ByT5 tags upalambhataḥ as a subanta of upalambha; a regular a-stem subanta would not yield -taḥ, and Sthiramati glosses it with ablatival -taḥ action expressions grahaṇataḥ and citrīkaraṇataḥ, so I analyze it as an indeclinable upalambhatas.
ByT5 gives the verb lemma as avasthā; per instruction I record the bare root sthā with prefix ava for avatiṣṭhate.

ByT5 gives sthāpayan with lemma sthāpay; I record the participial nominal stem sthāpayant for the subanta analysis, so that masculine nominative singular yields pausal sthāpayan and sandhied sthāpayann.

ByT5 gives lemma kaścit for kiñcit; I record the neuter singular stem/form kiñcit for the derivational analysis, while retaining the same neuter accusative singular construal supported by the commentary.
